overview.title: Thermal Vacuum Chamber Systems
overview.description: Deepvac develops standardised and custom-engineered thermal vacuum systems for aerospace qualification, environmental simulation, and research applications. Our chamber platforms address a range of test requirements — from established workflows using modular standard systems to fully bespoke configurations for complex mission profiles.

standardSeries.title: Standard Series
standardSeries.subtitle: Pre-Engineered TVAC Platforms
standardSeries.description: Modular thermal vacuum chamber systems with proven architectures, standardised interfaces, and repeatable performance for established test requirements. Available in cubic (T Series) and cylindrical (C Series) configurations.

customTvac.title: Custom TVAC
customTvac.subtitle: Application-Specific Chamber Engineering
customTvac.description: Fully bespoke thermal vacuum systems configured around mission-specific geometries, feedthrough requirements, thermal profiles, instrumentation needs, and integration constraints. Engineered from concept through commissioning.

configGuide.title: Standard Platform vs. Custom Configuration
configGuide.description: Use the comparison below to determine which approach best fits your test requirements, timeline, and integration constraints.

factors.label: Test Profile
factors.custom: Application-specific pressure, temperature, or cycling profiles

factors.label: Chamber Geometry
factors.standard: Standard cubic or cylindrical chamber volumes
factors.custom: Non-standard dimensions, access requirements, or payload geometries

factors.label: Feedthroughs & Interfaces
factors.standard: Pre-configured port layouts and feedthrough options
factors.custom: Custom interface engineering and specialised feedthrough integration

factors.label: Instrumentation
factors.custom: Application-specific instrumentation and data acquisition requirements

factors.label: Timeline
factors.standard: Shorter delivery timelines with proven designs

integrated.title: Beyond the Chamber
integrated.description: Deepvac provides engineering services across the full lifecycle — from control systems design and mechanical engineering to testing, retrofit, and subsystem integration.

items.q: What distinguishes a standard chamber from a custom configuration?
items.a: Standard Series chambers use pre-engineered designs with proven architectures, offering shorter lead times and established performance envelopes. Custom TVAC systems are engineered from concept to commissioning around application-specific requirements including geometry, thermal profile, instrumentation, and interface constraints.

items.q: Can standard chambers be modified for specific test requirements?
items.a: Standard Series platforms support a range of configuration options within their design envelope. For requirements beyond the standard platform scope, Deepvac offers custom engineering to develop application-specific solutions.

items.q: What support does Deepvac provide after chamber delivery?
items.a: Deepvac offers lifecycle services including preventive maintenance, repair, retrofit and modernisation, control system upgrades, and subsystem integration support for all chamber systems.

items.q: Does Deepvac provide testing services alongside chamber systems?
items.a: Yes. Deepvac offers thermal vacuum testing services including test campaign execution, data acquisition, and engineering support for qualification, validation, and development programs.

cta.title: Discuss Your Chamber Requirements
cta.description: Whether you need a modular standard platform or a custom-engineered TVAC system, Deepvac can help define the right configuration for your application.

standardSeries.title: Pre-Engineered TVAC Platforms
standardSeries.description: Standardised thermal vacuum chamber platforms with modular configurations and repeatable performance for aerospace qualification, environmental simulation, and research testing.

tSeries.title: T Series TVAC
tSeries.description: Compact cubic thermal vacuum chamber platforms designed for repeatable environmental simulation, subsystem validation, and laboratory workflows. The T Series combines controlled thermal and vacuum conditions with a standardised architecture for research and qualification applications.
tSeries.descriptionExtended: The cubic chamber geometry provides straightforward payload access, efficient interior utilisation, and flexible fixture mounting. Standard configurations support vacuum performance down to {{vacuum}} and thermal operation down to {{minTemp}}, depending on the selected system setup.

features.label: Cubic Geometry
features.detail: Cube-based chamber layout for straightforward payload access, efficient internal volume usage, and flexible fixture integration.

features.label: Low Temperature Capability
features.detail: Configured for thermal operation down to {{minTemp}}, depending on system configuration and test setup.

features.label: Ultimate Vacuum
features.detail: Designed for vacuum performance down to {{vacuum}}, depending on chamber size and pumping package.

features.label: Control Integration
features.detail: Pre-engineered control system interface with data acquisition and automation support.

cSeries.title: C Series TVAC
cSeries.description: Cylindrical thermal vacuum chamber platforms designed for repeatable environmental simulation, subsystem qualification, and robust laboratory operation. The C Series combines controlled thermal and vacuum conditions with a standardised architecture for research and qualification applications.
cSeries.descriptionExtended: The cylindrical chamber geometry supports classic vacuum chamber layouts, efficient shroud integration, and a structurally efficient form factor. Standard configurations support vacuum performance down to {{vacuum}} and thermal operation down to {{minTemp}}, depending on the selected system setup.

features.label: Cylindrical Geometry
features.detail: Cylindrical chamber layout suited for uniform radial design, efficient shroud integration, and classic vacuum chamber configurations.

features.label: Low Temperature Capability
features.detail: Configured for thermal operation down to {{minTemp}}, depending on system configuration and test setup.

features.label: Ultimate Vacuum
features.detail: Designed for vacuum performance down to {{vacuum}}, depending on chamber size and pumping package.

features.label: Control Integration
features.detail: Pre-engineered control system interface with data acquisition and automation support.

applicationFit.title: Designed for Demanding Test Environments
applicationFit.description: Standard Series platforms serve a wide range of aerospace, research, and industrial test applications.

beyondStandard.title: Need a Custom Configuration?
beyondStandard.description: When test requirements exceed the standard platform scope, including non-standard geometries, specialised feedthrough configurations, or application-specific thermal profiles, Deepvac offers fully custom-engineered TVAC solutions.

brochure.title: Deepvac Standard Series Catalogue
brochure.description: Explore the Deepvac Standard Series in more detail, including common technical characteristics, chamber configurations, control system features, and optional upgrades.

items.q: What chamber sizes are available in the Standard Series?
items.a: The Standard Series is available in both cubic and cylindrical chamber formats across a range of chamber volumes. Specific dimensions are selected according to the required test envelope, payload size, and integration needs.

items.q: What vacuum level can Standard Series chambers achieve?
items.a: Standard Series chambers are designed for vacuum performance down to {{vacuum}}, depending on chamber volume, pumping configuration, and application requirements.

items.q: What thermal control options are available?
items.a: Standard Series platforms include integrated thermal shroud and platen systems supporting controlled heating and cooling cycles. Minimum temperature capability can be configured down to {{minTemp}}, depending on the selected system configuration.

items.q: How are Standard Series chambers delivered and commissioned?
items.a: Deepvac provides full delivery, installation, and commissioning support including system integration, acceptance testing, and operator training.

cta.title: Discuss Your Test Requirements
cta.description: Contact Deepvac to identify the right Standard Series configuration for your application.

customTvac.title: Application-Specific Thermal Vacuum Engineering
customTvac.description: When standard chamber platforms cannot meet your test requirements, Deepvac develops fully custom thermal vacuum systems — engineered from concept through commissioning around your specific geometry, thermal profile, instrumentation, and integration constraints.
customTvac.heroCaption: Custom thermal vacuum chamber — engineered, integrated, and commissioned by Deepvac.

projectDrivers.title: When Standard Isn't Enough
projectDrivers.description: Custom thermal vacuum systems are engineered when your application demands capabilities, geometries, or integration requirements beyond the scope of standardised chamber platforms.

items.title: Non-Standard Geometries
items.description: Test articles that exceed standard chamber dimensions or require specialised access, orientation, or mounting configurations.

items.title: Specific Thermal Profiles
items.description: Applications demanding precise temperature ranges, ramp rates, uniformity requirements, or multi-zone thermal control.

items.title: Complex Feedthrough Requirements
items.description: Electrical, optical, fluid, or mechanical feedthrough configurations that exceed standard port layouts.

items.title: Contamination-Sensitive Testing
items.description: Hardware requiring low-outgassing environments, clean interfaces, and controlled material compatibility.

items.title: Specialised Control Architecture
items.description: Custom automation logic, advanced data acquisition, or integration with existing facility control systems.

items.title: Subsystem & Interface Constraints
items.description: Integration with existing infrastructure, specific utility connections, or project-defined mechanical and electrical interfaces.

midCta.title: Have a Specific Test Requirement?
midCta.description: Deepvac's engineering team can help evaluate your requirements and develop a system concept.

configDimensions.title: Configurable System Dimensions
configDimensions.description: Every custom TVAC system is defined across multiple engineering dimensions to match your specific application requirements.

process.title: From Concept to Commissioning
process.description: Deepvac supports the full development lifecycle of custom thermal vacuum systems — from initial requirements discussion through engineering, manufacturing, and installation.

steps.title: Requirements Discussion
steps.description: Technical consultation to understand your test requirements, payload characteristics, operational constraints, and integration context.

steps.title: Concept & System Design
steps.description: Engineering concept development including chamber geometry, thermal architecture, vacuum design, instrumentation, and control system specification.

steps.title: Detailed Engineering
steps.description: Detailed mechanical, electrical, and control system design with documentation, interface specifications, and design review milestones.

steps.title: Manufacturing & Assembly
steps.description: Precision manufacturing, subsystem assembly, and integration with quality control and testing throughout the build process.

steps.title: Commissioning & Support
steps.description: Delivery, installation, acceptance testing, operator training, and transition to lifecycle support services.

crossLinks.standardSeriesDescription: For established test requirements, Deepvac's Standard Series offers pre-engineered T Series and C Series platforms.
crossLinks.engineeringServicesDescription: Deepvac provides control systems design, mechanical engineering, testing services, and lifecycle support alongside chamber development.

items.q: When should I consider a custom TVAC system instead of a standard platform?
items.a: A custom configuration is appropriate when your test requirements include non-standard chamber dimensions, specific thermal profiles, complex feedthrough or instrumentation needs, contamination-sensitive hardware, or integration with existing facility infrastructure that exceeds the scope of standard platform options.

items.q: What information does Deepvac need to develop a custom system concept?
items.a: A productive initial discussion typically covers test article characteristics, required pressure and temperature ranges, thermal cycling requirements, feedthrough and instrumentation needs, facility constraints, and project timeline. Deepvac can guide this requirements definition process.

items.q: What is the typical timeline for a custom TVAC project?
items.a: Project timelines depend on system complexity, component lead times, and engineering scope. Deepvac provides project-specific timelines during the concept development phase.

items.q: Does Deepvac provide control systems for custom chambers?
items.a: Yes. Deepvac designs and integrates custom control architectures including automation logic, user interfaces, data acquisition, and instrumentation integration as part of the system delivery.

items.q: Can Deepvac integrate custom chambers with existing facility infrastructure?
items.a: Yes. Interface engineering and facility integration are core elements of Deepvac's custom chamber development process, including utility connections, safety systems, and control system interoperability.

items.q: What lifecycle support is available for custom systems?
items.a: Deepvac provides comprehensive lifecycle support including preventive maintenance, repair services, control system upgrades, retrofit and modernisation, and subsystem integration for all custom-engineered systems.

cta.title: Ready to Discuss Your Custom TVAC Project?
cta.description: Contact Deepvac to start the technical consultation process and define the right system architecture for your application.

thermalVision.title: Deepvac Thermal Vision
thermalVision.heroDescription: Pressure-tight thermal camera integration for thermal vacuum chambers. Continuous infrared imaging under high-vacuum and extreme temperature conditions — with active thermal management, PID-controlled operation, and full chamber integration engineering.
thermalVision.splitHeading: Integrated Thermal Imaging for TVAC Systems
thermalVision.bodyParagraph1: Commercially available infrared cameras cannot operate directly in high-vacuum and extreme-temperature environments without dedicated engineering measures. Deepvac Thermal Vision addresses this limitation through a pressure-tight enclosure with an IR-transparent viewport, enabling continuous, calibrated thermal imaging of test articles inside thermal vacuum chambers.
thermalVision.bodyParagraph2: The system includes an active cooling and optional heating circuit that maintains the camera within its specified operating temperature range under all chamber conditions. A PID-based temperature controller manages the thermal regulation and can be integrated into existing chamber control architectures. Deepvac provides complete integration engineering — enclosure design, thermal management, viewport specification, and mechanical interface — for both new TVAC installations and retrofit of existing systems.

features.label: Pressure-Tight Camera Enclosure
features.detail: Sealed stainless-steel housing with IR-transparent viewport. Maintains atmospheric pressure around the camera sensor while the chamber operates under high vacuum.

features.label: Active Thermal Management
features.detail: Integrated cooling circuit with optional heating maintains the camera within its safe operating temperature range throughout all chamber thermal cycles.

features.label: Temperature-Controlled Operation
features.detail: PID-based temperature regulation ensures stable imaging conditions. Controller interfaces with existing chamber automation and data acquisition systems.

features.label: New Build & Retrofit Integration
features.detail: Mechanical and thermal interface engineering for integration into new TVAC installations or retrofit of existing thermal vacuum chambers. Single-source delivery.
